title: Selling Ebooks Without Stripe: An Honest Side-by-Side Breakdown: The Honest Answer
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal and Stripe block creators in dozens of countries. Here is the infrastructure that works everywhere they don't.

content.text: Are you a digital creator in Nigeria, Ghana, or Pakistan struggling to sell ebooks because PayPal won't let you in? You're not alone. Every day, thousands of creators face the same problem – their country is blocked by popular payment processors like Stripe, Gumroad, or Payhip. This post cuts through the noise to give you an honest side-by-side breakdown of your options. We'll explore the real costs, limitations, and tradeoffs of each choice, so you can make an informed decision about how to sell your ebooks online without getting stuck.
What Each Option Actually Offers

When you're excluded from PayPal, Stripe, or Gumroad, you're often left with limited options. One choice is to use a traditional payment processor like NOWPayments, which supports 300+ coins and doesn't require KYC or a buyer account. However, this means you'll need to handle delivery, receipts, and analytics on your own, which can be time-consuming. Another option is to build a custom solution, but this requires technical expertise and can be costly. Our Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) provides a permanent structural alternative, with automated delivery, payment verification, PDF receipts, and real-time analytics, all without the need for a payment processor account.
How They Handle Restricted Countries

PayPal, Stripe, and Gumroad have strict policies against allowing creators from certain countries to sell their ebooks. This is due to their focus on mitigating risk and complying with anti-money laundering regulations. In contrast, NOWPayments and our Full Stack Bundle don't have these restrictions, making them a better fit for creators in restricted countries. Our solution is designed to be global, with no KYC requirements or buyer accounts needed, giving you the freedom to sell your ebooks to anyone, anywhere in the world.
Payment Speed, Fees, and Real Costs

When you're using a traditional payment processor, you often face high fees and slow payment speeds. NOWPayments charges a flat 0.5% fee for domestic transactions and 2% for international transactions. In contrast, our Full Stack Bundle eliminates these fees, allowing you to keep 100% of your earnings. Additionally, our automated delivery and payment verification ensure that you receive your payments quickly, usually within minutes of a sale.
Traditional payment processors often lack automation and delivery capabilities, leaving you to manually handle these tasks.
Automation and Delivery Capabilities

The risk of platform dependency is asymmetric in a specific way that most creators underestimate. When PayPal or Stripe is working, everything seems fine. When it stops working — through suspension, policy change, or geographic restriction enforcement — you lose access to your revenue channel instantly, often with funds already inside the platform that you cannot withdraw. Rebuilding on a new platform takes weeks. The income gap during that period is real money lost. Crypto-native infrastructure removes this asymmetric risk. Your wallet cannot be suspended. Your GitHub Pages store cannot be taken down by a payment platform's compliance team. The infrastructure is under your control from day one, which means no single external decision can cut off your income.
Which One to Choose Based on Your Situation

Ultimately, the choice between NOWPayments and our Full Stack Bundle depends on your specific needs and circumstances. If you're looking for a simple, low-cost solution with minimal automation, NOWPayments might be the better choice. However, if you want a more advanced, automated solution that eliminates fees and provides real-time analytics, our Full Stack Bundle is the way to go. With our solution, you can sell ebooks online without the hassle and limitations of traditional payment processors.

Can I use Stripe if I am in Nigeria, Ghana, or Pakistan? Unfortunately, no. Stripe has strict policies against allowing creators from certain countries to sell their ebooks.
